items.summary: Harry Connick Jr's new symphony, Elaboratio, is filled with musical tributes to his mother Anita. Orchestra Victoria and Australian conductor Jessica Gethin are bringing those memories to life at Carnegie Hall.

items.title: Air France and Airbus found guilty of involuntary manslaughter for Rio-Paris flight crash
items.summary: France's national airline Air France and Airbus have both been found guilty of involuntary manslaughter over a 2009 crash that killed 228 people.

items.title: Another group of Australians with IS links leave camp in Syria for Australia
items.summary: The ABC has seen buses carrying the group, which left the al-Roj camp on Thursday afternoon, local time.

items.title: The Mushroom Tapes, Mad Mabel triumph at Australian Book Industry Awards
items.summary: In May 2025, Helen Garner, Chloe Hooper and Sarah Krasnostein, three literary true-crime writers, headed to Erin Patterson's trial. The resulting book just won an Australian Book Industry Award.

items.title: Price denies agreeing migrants from Asia, Africa and Middle East are 'flooding' Australia
items.summary: Jacinta Nampijinpa Price has denied that she agreed with a podcaster saying Australia should stop 'flooding' Australia with migrants from Asia, Africa and the Middle East.

items.title: School in mouse-infested WA town closed after toxic bait spread on grounds
items.summary: Students of the Western Australian School of Agriculture in the WA town of Morawa are sent home and the school is closed after toxic mouse bait intended for paddocks is spread on the campus.

items.title: Thursday finance with Daniel Ziffer
items.summary: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

items.title: 'I've never been so confused': Raiders slam officials, fall to Dolphins in thriller
items.summary: The Raiders huffed and puffed but couldn't overcome the depleted Dolphins, who despite missing six key players through injury and Origin won a fourth-straight NRL game for the first time.

items.title: Tasmanian state budget 2026: Ratings agency S&P warns budget savings 'hard to achieve' as others react
items.summary: Credit rating agency S&P Global says Tasmania's budget repair ambitions face "high execution risk". The government aims to save $1.5 billion through cuts.

items.title: Pauline Hanson proposes new gas tax and public stake in drilling projects
items.summary: The One Nation leader wants a flat royalty on the production of new gas projects.

items.title: 'Emotional' Hawks kick off Tassie farewell as star Crow injured
items.summary: Blake Hardwick emerges the unlikeliest of heroes for Hawthorn as the Hawks record a nine-point win over Adelaide in Tasmania.

items.title: Man charged with five counts of rape after alleged break-in at Adelaide Hills home
items.summary: A man accused of breaking into an Adelaide Hills home and sexually assaulting a person unknown to him has been charged with multiple counts of rape, court documents show.

items.title: SpaceX files for largest IPO in history, with potential to make Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire
items.summary: Elon Musk's sprawling space exploration company, which also owns Starlink and xAI, was valued at $US1.25 trillion ($1.75 trillion) earlier this year, meaning Musk could become the world's first trillionaire if the offering goes ahead at that valuation.

items.title: Prison term looms for ex-AFL footballer Nick Stevens as victims tell court of impact
items.summary: A Victorian court hears former AFL player Nick Stevens was a blue-collar worker but committed a white-collar crime, ahead of his sentencing for defrauding six families in 2017.

items.title: Albanese's budget backlash tour spends little time with Coalition
items.summary: For a group of people who declared they were prepared to tackle a post-budget scare campaign, the government has looked anything but in control this week.

items.title: Man sentenced for killing Ian Welldon in Toowoomba truck and motorcycle crash
items.summary: The man had a "very high" level of methamphetamine in his blood when he crashed into the back of Ian Welldon's motorbike while failing to stop at a red light.

items.title: Isabelle Kelly returns to chase an Origin dynasty 10 years after breakthrough Blues win
items.summary: Ten years after playing in New South Wales's breakthrough victory over Queensland, Isabelle Kelly returns to the Gold Coast on the cusp of launching a Blues Origin dynasty.

items.title: NDIS integrity inquiry hears raft of alarming claims and calls for greater whistleblower protections
items.summary: A raft of alarming evidence has been aired at a parliamentary hearing into the integrity of the NDIS, with calls for the government to strengthen whistleblower protections.

items.title: Public transport to stay free for an additional year in Tasmania
items.summary: The Tasmanian government has committed $24 million in the state budget towards keeping buses and ferries free for one more year.

items.title: AFLW's 2026 fixture to feature women's and men's double-headers
items.summary: A series of double-headers involving the women's and men's competitions will take place after the AFLW season begins in August.

items.title: Coles investigates Apple gift card scam after Melbourne artist's viral complaint
items.summary: Coles is looking into a Melbourne mother's complaint about faulty Apple gift cards worth $2,000.

items.title: The Besties say violent podcast clip is 'just one example of what we deal with' as female creators
items.summary: A video circulated across social media of a group of men laughing about how one of them would "beat the f***" out of a group of female content creators.

items.title: Perth and WA council mergers are on the table again — here's what happened last time
items.summary: In 2015 the Barnett government abandoned plans to merge Perth councils — but now the idea has reared its head again. Here's what you need to know.

items.title: Tasmanian state budget 2026: Where are the cuts coming from?
items.summary: Treasurer Eric Abetz wants to find $1.47 billion in "efficiencies" in Tasmania's state budget. This is what's on the chopping block.

items.title: Tasmanian state budget 2026: Government to slash 1,700 jobs as Eric Abetz looks to rein in spending
items.summary: The Tasmanian government will slash 1,700 jobs through natural attrition and negotiated voluntary redundancies, as Treasurer Eric Abetz unveils his second state budget.

items.title: Arafura Rare Earths mine green-lit as federal government agrees to buy 500 tonnes of product per year

items.title: Government doing 'everything in our power' to keep SA safe from paedophile Peter Liddy
items.summary: The South Australian government has filed two applications to either ensure notorious paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy is indefinitely detained or the subject of an extended supervision order.

items.title: Jai Arrow's MND diagnosis shines spotlight on debilitating condition that has no cure
items.summary: As another rugby league player is diagnosed with motor neurone disease, we asked experts why so little is known about what causes it.

items.title: Family's story of dementia shines spotlight on Australia's leading cause of death
items.summary: Gwen Knox, from Western Australia, has written a play inspired by her family that she hopes starts important conversations about dementia and truth-telling.

items.title: Israeli minister's taunting of activists no surprise to those following his career
items.summary: Benjamin Netanyahu has condemned a video of his national security minister taunting activists, including Australians, who have been detained. But it's the prime minister who allowed this behaviour to percolate.

items.title: Liberal leader Angus Taylor spruiks gas extraction in Adelaide as SA party opposes fracking proposal
items.summary: After telling an Adelaide oil and gas conference "we need to get busy digging and drilling", Opposition Leader Angus Taylor bats away questions about the SA Liberal Party's resistance to fracking in the state's South East.

items.title: Peak body slams ministers' absence from Aboriginal housing and homelands conference as 'disgraceful'
items.summary: The Northern Territory's peak body for Aboriginal housing says it's "disgraceful" ministers from both levels of government have failed to attend a conference on the housing crisis.

items.title: Man convicted of Irma Palasics's death was previously jailed over million-dollar cigarette conspiracy
items.summary: New details have emerged about the violent 1999 home invasion that claimed Irma Palasics's life, including a separate million-dollar cigarette conspiracy that put one of her killers behind bars a decade later.

items.title: Woman accused of hit-and-run murder of Kiesha Thompson at Daisy Hill committed to stand trial
items.summary: Larissa Rita Mae-Leigh Sant is accused of planning to kill her then-partner's ex-girlfriend, Kiesha Thompson, before striking her with a car while she was walking.

items.title: Bail refused after guns, antisemitic material allegedly seized from Central Coast home
items.summary: A man has been refused bail after NSW Police allegedly seized antisemitic material, firearms and explosives from a home on the Central Coast.

items.title: Adelaide lawyer who drank 1.15L of wine before fatal hit-run gets reduced jail term on appeal
items.summary: An Adelaide lawyer who drank 1.1 litres of wine before killing a pedestrian in a fatal hit-run crash has won a reduced sentence on appeal — in part because she has a young child.

items.title: Kylie doco is a human portrait of pop royalty's resilience and reinvention
items.summary: "Our Kylie" gets candid in the three-part Netflix docuseries, grieving over IVF struggles, Michael Hutchence, and revealing she was diagnosed with cancer a second time in 2021.

items.title: Australia backs landmark UN climate change ruling as others try to block it
items.summary: Australia joins 140 other countries in passing a major United Nations resolution backing a landmark legal ruling on climate change, despite efforts by the United States, Russia and Saudi Arabia to sink it.

items.title: Australia's largest diphtheria outbreak prompts $7.2m federal vaccine blitz
items.summary: The federal government has stepped in to address the largest diphtheria outbreak since records began, unveiling a multi-million dollar package for more vaccines and workers to deliver them.

items.title: Murder accused John Yalu told police a pensioner was 'pressuring' him to amputate his leg
items.summary: A jury is deciding whether John Yalu, 41, committed murder when he used a circular saw to amputate the leg of a pensioner who had been "pressuring" him to remove the limb in exchange for $5,000.

items.title: Football Australia posts record losses as plan to axe 20 per cent of staff revealed
items.summary: About 20 per cent of Football Australia staffers face the axe as soccer's governing body posts record losses that doubled last financial year's numbers.

items.title: Earthquakes hit near regional Queensland town twice in four days
items.summary: Experts say this week's seismic activity is unusual, but the region has a pattern of large and clustered earthquakes.

items.title: Five reasons Stephen Colbert is one of the most important satirists in American history
items.summary: The best satirists do more than entertain. They influence public discourse and leave lasting marks on political life.

items.title: Unemployment rate jumps to 4.5 per cent, driven by female unemployment
items.summary: All four major banks now expect the RBA to keep the cash rate on hold at its next meeting on June 16, as the unemployment rate spikes to 4.5 per cent.

items.title: Tech giant Nvidia reports growth of 85 per cent due to the construction of AI factories
items.summary: The world's most valuable company, tech giant Nvidia, has reported a record revenue but investors are not impressed with second-quarter sales forecasts.

items.title: Joey Chestnut to defend hot-dog-eating crown on probation after battery plea
items.summary: The competitive-eating sensation was accused of slapping a man across the face during a late night out at an Indiana bar, according to Us Weekly.

items.title: Students return to Cairns high school as police revoke emergency declaration
items.summary: Police have revoked an emergency declaration under the Public Safety Preservation Act after a Cairns high school was evacuated following reports of a threat.

items.title: On the eve of Stephen Colbert's final Late Show, America is asking: Can late night survive the Trump administration?
items.summary: Like other checks and balances on presidential power, late-night talk shows — once ratings juggernauts, woven into the fabric of American culture — are being stress-tested. Here's how they became a staging ground for the battle over free speech.

items.title: Birth choices dwindle as new laws push Geraldton's last private midwife to press pause in WA
items.summary: Soon-to-be parents in regional WA will have less choice over where and how they give birth as care options diminish, midwives say.

items.title: Prince William goes wild as Aston Villa claims Europa League title and ends 30-year drought
items.summary: In front of a joyful future king of England, Aston Villa coach Unai Emery reaffirmed his status as the king of the Europa League as Villa beat Freiburg 3-0 in Istanbul to secure Emery a record-extending fifth title in the Europa League.

items.title: What does broken heart syndrome feel like?
items.summary: Experts say broken heart syndrome accounts for about two per cent of patients presenting with symptoms similar to a heart attack.

items.title: Outback landholders opening gate to large-scale renewable projects
items.summary: If you mention renewable energy to agricultural producers in many regional communities, you will likely ruffle some feathers. But in far west NSW, landholders are showing preliminary support for a planned expansion of solar and wind energy assets.

items.title: Retiring NRL star Jai Arrow says he will fight after shock MND diagnosis
items.summary: Jai Arrow reveals that MND has already affected his speech after announcing yesterday he was stepping away from the NRL after his shock diagnosis.

items.title: Abrolhos Islands coral reef shows resistance to climate bleaching, study finds
items.summary: In the midst of a mass coral bleaching event along the West Australian coast, a group of scientists have discovered a section of reef that has been able to withstand the heat.

items.title: Congo football team cancels Kinshasa World Cup farewell as Ebola outbreak concerns grow
items.summary: The Congo football team cancels its farewell in Kinshasa as the US clarified what the rules would be for players and staff entering the country for the World Cup.

items.title: Gen Zs who take a lottery ticket approach to investing will pay more tax under CGT changes
items.summary: Gen Zs who take a lottery ticket approach to investing in the stock market will likely pay more tax under the capital gains tax changes being proposed by the government.

items.title: Iran examining US proposal as Donald Trump says talks in 'final stages'
items.summary: Iran says it is examining the latest US proposal received from mediator Pakistan in talks to end the Middle East war, despite the two sides trading threats over resuming attacks.

items.title: NT health authorities MIA as diphtheria spreads across Indigenous remote communities
items.summary: As diphtheria spreads across four different Australian jurisdictions in the largest outbreak since records began, public health messaging in the NT — the region most impacted by the serious disease — has been hard to come by.

items.title: Paul Keating backs CGT changes as 'structurally sound' — as it happened
items.summary: Former prime minister Paul Keating says the government's changes to capital gains tax are "structurally sound" and seek to "arrest" the growth in house prices.

items.title: Australian rugby 7s co-captain Isabella Nasser inspired by 2016 Olympics victory
items.summary: When Isabella Nasser tuned in to watch the 2016 Rio Olympics as a 13-year-old, she had little idea of the pathway her life was about to take.

items.title: Is Canberra's CBD unsafe? It depends who you ask
items.summary: ACT industry groups say drug use and widespread construction are among issues eroding feelings of safety.

items.title: X asks court to dismiss Andrew and Tristan Tate's bid to unmask anonymous critics
items.summary: Misogynist influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate have struck a hurdle in their bid to unmask anonymous users they allege have defamed them, with social media platform X asking a Florida court to dismiss the motion.

items.title: Strathmerton fears it will become 'ghost town' as Bega Cheese factory closure nears
items.summary: Bega Cheese's Strathmerton factory, which employed more than 300 workers, is set to close its doors on June 30, leaving residents in the small Victorian town wondering what's next.

items.title: Photographer accused of stealing Bondi terror attack victim Peter Meagher's camera
items.summary: A photographer who allegedly stole a camera from a Bondi terror attack victim before pawning it days later has been charged.

items.title: Scott Pendlebury's 433rd game simply the latest in a long line of Collingwood star's AFL records
items.summary: Scott Pendlebury is set to be the proud holder of the AFL's games record, but it's only the latest in a long line of records held by the Collingwood icon.
